title: Examples, Delimiters, Templates, and Structured Output · Prompt Lens: Structured Sections
summary: Stabilize a classification and extraction prompt across varied inputs. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt structured sections increment in a tested prompt template with diverse examples. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate instructions, context, examples, and variable data with clear headings or provider-appropriate tags.
Include counterexamples and near misses when category boundaries are material.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Predict Structured Sections
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt examples structure. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.text: Separate instructions, context, examples, and variable data with clear headings or provider-appropriate tags. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Worked case pattern

steps.title: Build Structured Sections evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-structured-sections-1fzyoh" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Structured Sections into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-structured-sections-1fzyoh
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

stimulus.title: Structured Sections failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.text: Decorative structure improves performance regardless of content.

lines.label: required

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Structured Sections, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional.

steps.title: Defend the Structured Sections model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Structured Sections applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate instructions, context, examples, and variable data with clear headings or provider-appropriate tags.
